segments.title: Kaiyo Maru reconstructed from a wreck
segments.summary: At the Kaiyo Maru Memorial Museum, a reconstructed ship presents the archaeology of the wreck from which it was rebuilt.
segments.body: Dr. Clarke introduces the Kaiyo Maru as an unusual museum ship because it is a reconstruction rather than an intact surviving vessel. The original ship was wrecked, and the museum incorporates archaeological material recovered from it into the visitor experience. He explains that visitors can walk around the reconstruction while seeing that archaeological evidence. The ship therefore offers a useful example of how maritime archaeology can support the interpretation and reconstruction of a lost vessel. Weather may restrict access to the deck, but the museum remains the focus of this stop on the Japan trip.

evidence.note: The transcript identifies the vessel as a reconstructed wreck and says visitors can see the archaeology associated with it.
Dr. Clarke identifies the Kaiyo Maru Memorial Museum and describes it as the final planned ship visit of the trip.
